112333.explanation: It’s about the quality of the executive board. Gender does not matter to the DOP.

112334.explanation: Referendum is not the participation tool to involve or consult residents. According to DOP, on important issues you should work together with residents from the start to create a plan.

112337.explanation: DOP supports the generation of sustainable energy, but to what extent common financial support is necessary remains a question.

112338.explanation: DOP wants to support and facilitate entrepreneurship within the environmental plans. So if this fits within the environmental plans, it can be done.

112341.explanation: DOP believes that consideration should be given to smog alerts and/or guidelines from Stookwijzer.nl.

112346.explanation: Residents entitled to social assistance benefits can additionally use the DijkenWaardPas for this. They can use this pass more broadly than just for a sports club.

112347.explanation: DOP considers a local broadcaster important and has a positive attitude towards additional support if this proves necessary.

112349.explanation: This is an important point for DOP: every child must have access to music and sports.

112352.explanation: The BOA (special enforcement officers) team in our municipality is well staffed and is professionally developing. When new tasks arise, we will assess what is needed.

112353.explanation: DOP considers the use of camera surveillance necessary if the feeling of safety needs to be increased in certain places.

112355.explanation: Society is hardening and unfortunately we increasingly see violent reactions towards BOAs doing their work. DOP believes this should be an option if safety professionals see the necessity.

112356.explanation: DOP finds it logical that a system should prioritize housing seekers from the own municipality.

112358.explanation: DOP believes the municipality should adopt a facilitating role here, but should not play a role in financing this through subsidies.

112359.explanation: This is not or hardly an issue in our municipality. The municipality has no HBO (higher professional education) or WO (university) education.

112360.explanation: Obligating work does not encourage positive contribution to society. It is precisely doing it yourself in the form of volunteer work and training that gives people the confidence to contribute with their knowledge to society.

112364.explanation: The municipality has developed a charging station strategy which, according to DOP, must continue to be followed.

112365.explanation: If lowering the speed limit within built-up areas to 30 km/h is a solution to increase traffic safety, DOP believes this can be applied. Not all roads and not mandatory everywhere is currently considered.

112369.explanation: DOP believes that quality and price must be balanced, including in youth care.

112370.explanation: DOP believes this should be left to organizations dedicated to this. The municipality should adopt a facilitating role for initiatives from these organizations.

112373.explanation: According to DOP, this is not an item for our municipality.

112376.explanation: DOP believes there must be a balanced distribution of social rental homes, taking into account national legal agreements. Priority or always receiving priority is not included here.

112378.explanation: DOP believes we must be very cautious about this. We have learned from the past.

112383.explanation: DOP is fine with retaining the existing coffeeshop, even if it has to relocate. We would not quickly support additional coffeeshops.

112384.explanation: DOP supports this approach. This is done area-wise, so if there is a project with too few social rental homes, compensation in another project will be necessary.

112386.explanation: DOP believes the municipality should be open to this.

112387.explanation: DOP considers this an important position. This has already been initiated by us.

112392.explanation: DOP believes the municipality can better focus on regional cooperation regarding HBO (e.g., Alkmaar) while our municipality should excel in MBO institutions. Being the MBO capital of North Holland would be fitting for Dijk and Waard.

112394.explanation: A seniors council does not seem a valid participation instrument to DOP.

112333.explanation: Quality and suitability are leading. Diversity is important, but fixed quotas should never be more important than the right person in the right place.

112334.explanation: Residents must be involved early and seriously. Local Dijk and Waard wants to investigate a referendum.

112337.explanation: Local energy initiatives contribute to sustainability and support. The municipality supports this where it is feasible and affordable.

112338.explanation: Employment and economy are important. Per location, it must be carefully weighed what fits the environment and accessibility.

112341.explanation: Freedom and housing pleasure remain important. We continue to focus on conscious use and limiting nuisance. We certainly do not completely prohibit it, but we want to make use of the stookwijzer.

112346.explanation: Participation must be possible for everyone. Sport strengthens health and social cohesion. We can make this possible with the Dijk and Waard pass.

112347.explanation: A strong local broadcaster strengthens democracy and information provision in the municipality.

112349.explanation: Culture and talent development are important for the development of children. If a small contribution helps, Local Dijk and Waard is willing to contribute.

112352.explanation: Effectiveness is more important than numbers. Smart deployment and collaboration are leading. We can potentially attract new enforcement officers if the situation requires it.

112353.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety in places where it is needed.

112355.explanation: De-escalation and professionalism are paramount in enforcement. The current situation in Dijk and Waard does not yet require a baton.

112356.explanation: Local connection and flow-through are important for livable neighborhoods. Dijk and Waard is a municipality where residents want to continue living. We must keep making this possible.

112358.explanation: Transformation is possible, but not routinely with subsidies. Market and location are decisive. The municipality should be open to transformation where possible, but only in a facilitating role. Additionally, the (future) demand from entrepreneurs should be leading.

112359.explanation: The focus is on structural housing for local residents. Student housing is not relevant here and there is no demand for it.

112360.explanation: Participation is important, but obligation without customization is inhumane. Not everyone is mentally or physically capable.

112364.explanation: A balance is needed between accessibility and charging options. We already have a good charging network. Also consider other charging options, such as charging spots where non-EVs are also allowed to park.

112365.explanation: Lower speeds increase traffic safety and livability. So we want to do this where possible. But there are also places where it is not yet necessary. We do not want to get ahead of ourselves there.

112369.explanation: Because quality and effectiveness of youth care should weigh more than just the price. Excessive focus on costs increases pressure on youth care and can ultimately lead to higher social costs.

112370.explanation: Period poverty should not be a barrier to participation. By offering free menstrual products through existing schemes and well-distributed locations, the municipality reaches residents easily and effectively.

112373.explanation: Visitors may contribute to facilities. A slight increase in the tourist tax is a good tool.

112376.explanation: Priority is a means, not a goal. We want to meet the legal task. This requires sufficient construction in the coming years so that priority no longer needs to be granted.

112378.explanation: Careful multiple use of space can contribute to sustainability. Also on agricultural land.

112383.explanation: Lokaal Dijk en Waard does not want to block new initiatives beforehand. Whether a coffee shop fits depends on location, safety, nuisance, and support. With clear rules and good enforcement, a balanced decision can be made in each situation.

112384.explanation: Affordable housing is a core task. A solid share of social rental is needed to offer prospects to starters, seniors, and families. However, customization per project remains important to keep neighborhoods mixed and livable.

112386.explanation: Room rental can increase pressure on neighborhoods and affect livability. Lokaal Dijk en Waard opts for control over housing and prevents situations of overcrowding and nuisance.

112387.explanation: Meeting places are important, but fixed standards do not suit every village. Lokaal Dijk en Waard chooses customization: what is needed and feasible differs per village.

112389.explanation: Public space is limited. More parking spaces often mean less room for greenery, housing, or social interaction. Lokaal Dijk en Waard opts for smarter use of existing space rather than automatic expansion.

112392.explanation: Education is important, but establishing an applied university requires regional coordination and realism. Lokaal Dijk en Waard prefers to focus on good connections with existing education in the region.

112394.explanation: This was recently established in the municipality of Dijk en Waard. Lokaal Dijk en Waard wants to give this a chance.

112333.explanation: For VVD Dijk en Waard, quality and suitability count, not gender. Governing is done with the best people, not with quotas.

112334.explanation: Governance requires clear choices and responsibility. This lies with an elected municipal council, where it is important that residents are involved in a timely and good manner.

112337.explanation: Sustainability must remain voluntary and affordable. The municipality facilitates but does not distribute subsidies.

112338.explanation: VVD Dijk en Waard wants a business-friendly municipality. Distribution centers are welcome, provided they are well integrated.

112341.explanation: Residents decide for themselves how they heat their homes. We do not want a general ban but rely on common sense and clear rules in case of nuisance.

112346.explanation: Sport is important, but the municipality does not automatically pay memberships. Support is targeted and temporary. We encourage personal responsibility.

112347.explanation: Independent journalism is important, but extra structural funding from the municipality is not necessary. Public money should go to core tasks, not extra subsidies.

112349.explanation: Education is important, but not everything needs to be subsidized by the municipality. Schools and parents make these choices themselves.

112352.explanation: More enforcement officers on the streets means more safety. Visible enforcement brings peace to neighborhoods.

112353.explanation: In places with nuisance or crime, camera surveillance is an effective tool to protect residents.

112355.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to do their work safely. A baton is sometimes necessary for their own protection.

112356.explanation: Social rental homes are primarily for residents of Dijk en Waard.

112358.explanation: We accelerate housing construction by fewer rules and faster building, not by subsidies for complex renovations. Entrepreneurs need this space to grow.

112359.explanation: Dijk en Waard focuses on starters, families, and seniors. Student housing has no priority here.

112360.explanation: Those who can contribute, contribute. That is fair and helps people move forward.

112364.explanation: More charging points must not come at the expense of parking spaces for residents. We install charging stations only where it makes sense and does not cause extra parking pressure.

112365.explanation: A general 30 km/h rule slows traffic flow. We choose customization: where necessary for safety, where possible for smooth traffic.

112369.explanation: Price is important but should not be decisive. In youth care, we primarily look at quality and what really works for children and families, with attention to affordability.

112370.explanation: Not everything important needs to be provided free by the municipality. We prefer to assist residents in a targeted way through existing support rather than new, general schemes.

112373.explanation: A limited increase in the tourist tax is reasonable since it is currently low. This way, visitors contribute to facilities in the municipality without disadvantaging our residents.

112376.explanation: Status holders do not receive priority. Social rental must be distributed fairly.

112378.explanation: Agricultural land is for food production. We first make use of roofs and infrastructure.

112383.explanation: We do not want extra nuisance and crime in our neighborhoods.

112384.explanation: Social rental housing is important, but a fixed percentage does not always match local needs. We choose a mix of housing per project that suits Dijk and Waard and allows for faster construction.

112386.explanation: To address the housing shortage, various forms of living must be possible. Subdivision into rooms can help make better use of existing homes, as long as livability and safety in the neighborhood are maintained.

112387.explanation: Not every village needs its own community center. We assess what works per village and only invest where there is a real need.

112389.explanation: The municipality is growing, which requires sufficient parking spaces. Living, working, and accessibility must remain in balance.

112392.explanation: We focus on strengthening vocational education (MBO) and craftsmanship. This better aligns with the jobs and entrepreneurs in Dijk and Waard.

112394.explanation: We listen to seniors through existing consultation and participation forms. A separate senior council adds little to this.

112333.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that the municipal executive has good representation of both men and women, as this will lead to better representation of our population. Furthermore, we should also strive for more diversity.

112334.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers participation very important. However, referenda are not always the best form of participation.

112337.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA values local initiatives that contribute to the energy transition highly. If financial support is needed, we are happy to provide it.

112338.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that as long as we face heavy traffic pressure on current main roads, we should advocate against causing even more traffic congestion.

112341.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes the health disadvantages for our residents from improper use of fireplaces outweigh the benefits.

112346.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers sports very important. The possibility of free membership already exists through the Dijk and Waard Pass, which must definitely be maintained.

112347.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers the local broadcaster of great importance.

112349.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA regards music as very important for the development of children.

112352.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes there are enough enforcement officers (boa's) active in Dijk and Waard. We should rather focus on prevention.

112353.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is cautious about more camera surveillance because it infringes privacy, can lead to mass surveillance, and reinforce discrimination. True safety is achieved through prevention, trust, and investing in people, not by monitoring everyone.

112355.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opposes arming enforcement officers (boa's) with batons because this can lead to unnecessary escalation and violence. Enforcement should focus on de-escalation, trust, and cooperation, not increasing coercion tools. The monopoly on violence belongs to the police.

112356.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opposes municipal priority in social rental housing because this undermines solidarity. Everyone should have equal opportunities for housing, regardless of municipal boundaries; fair distribution in the region prevents arbitrariness and exclusion.

112358.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers it important to make it easier to convert empty offices into homes. We think more in terms of simplifying zoning plan adjustments than providing subsidies.

112359.explanation: Dijk and Waard is not a student city. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA finds it more important to invest energy in good permanent housing for young people.

112360.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opposes mandatory social work as a condition for welfare benefits because this can lead to coercion and displacement of paid work. Assistance should focus on trust, customization, and real job opportunities, not on compulsory counter-performances.

112364.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports more parking spaces with charging points for electric cars to stimulate sustainable driving, reduce CO₂ emissions, and make the transition to clean transport accessible and practical for everyone.

112365.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports 30 km/h speed limits within built-up areas because it increases traffic safety, provides clear rules for all road users, and contributes to less pollution and noise, making neighborhoods more livable and sustainable.

112369.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opposes price as the main criterion for youth care. Quality, safety, and effectiveness are more important so that children receive the right support. Cheaper options must never come at the expense of care and well-being.

112370.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports free tampons and sanitary pads because menstruation should not be a luxury. Everyone must have access to hygiene products, poverty is reduced, and equality between youth and adults is increased.

112373.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that in the coming period we should focus on keeping the municipality financially healthy. A discussion about increasing the tourist tax - with an expected revenue of €160,000 in 2025, 0.05% of the budget - distracts from the main issues.

112376.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that the agreed rules for allocating social rental housing are clear, fair, and just. The long waiting times are mainly caused by too few social rental homes being built.

112378.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers the installation of solar panels an important topic. Roofs of houses, residential complexes, institutions, and companies are suitable for this. According to GroenLinks-PvdA, this also applies to agricultural land.

112383.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA sees no reason to treat the establishment of a new coffeeshop differently than the establishment of any other new sales point.

112384.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that 40% of the newly built homes should be social rental housing. The long waiting times for housing seekers at the SVNK show that demand for social rental housing is (still) high.

112386.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to facilitate slumlords. At the same time, renting out vacant rooms in a home can be a temporary solution for urgent housing seekers.

112387.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA agrees with this statement. As far as we are concerned, the statement applies not only to the villages in Dijk and Waard but also to the various neighborhoods (in Heerhugowaard).

112389.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA commits to more and better public transport, good and safe cycling paths. Facilitating car use by building parking lots in the cores does not fit this vision.

112392.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that an initiative from a school board wishing to start a university of applied sciences (HBO faculty) in Dijk and Waard should be positively assessed by the municipality. This is different from lobbying. The initiative should come from the school board.

112394.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA recognizes that all parties participating in the municipal elections pay attention to the specific needs of seniors in Dijk and Waard. In our opinion, a separate Seniors Council contributes too little to the decision-making process from a democratic perspective.

112333.explanation: The best candidate with knowledge and skills should be nominated. Whether this candidate is male or female is not a criterion.

112334.explanation: Opinions are gathered through participation evenings.

112337.explanation: Stimulating and subsidizing insulation measures for old homes through information and subsidy opportunities.

112338.explanation: Brings employment opportunities for our residents.

112341.explanation: Wood smoke is harmful to human health.

112346.explanation: Sports contribution can be arranged via the Dijk en Waard pass.

112347.explanation: Subsidy is already arranged by Dijk en Waard.

112349.explanation: Schools create their own program. If music lessons are included, these will also be provided.

112352.explanation: Continuous investment must be made in the BOAs and youth workers who are visibly present in the neighborhoods, villages, and shopping centers. Dijk en Waard should be a safe living environment.

112353.explanation: Improving safety is an important task for the municipality from the safety region.

112355.explanation: BOAs in Dijk en Waard themselves indicate when other resources are needed. Dijk en Waard will follow national policy.

112356.explanation: For some new housing projects, residents of Dijk en Waard will receive priority treatment over people from outside the municipality.

112358.explanation: Suitable for starters. These starters currently have to wait far too long for housing.

112359.explanation: They currently have to wait far too long before they become eligible.

112360.explanation: It is good to gain experience and to expand the network. That ensures better progression to paid work.

112364.explanation: The policy in the Netherlands is that there should be fewer petrol cars. So more charging stations can help with this.

112365.explanation: Where necessary and possible, the traffic environment must be set up as a 30 km zone with appropriate traffic-calming measures.

112369.explanation: Youth care is necessary, but costs must always be considered.

112370.explanation: Not a task for the municipality. Possibly the food bank can play a role here.

112373.explanation: Tourists may well contribute to what they receive.

112378.explanation: No solar meadows in our municipality to preserve and respect the existing rural landscape. We actively focus on placing solar panels on, among other things, roofs of existing houses and buildings, especially in the industrial area.

112383.explanation: One coffeeshop is tolerated in the municipality of Dijk en Waard; any expansion would only cause more nuisance.

112384.explanation: 30% social rental + owner-occupied housing is already a rule in our municipality.

112386.explanation: The Ministry wants to amend the law on subletting due to the housing shortage.

112387.explanation: Needed for young and old. Against loneliness, for knowledge transfer.

112389.explanation: We are basically in favor of increasing the number of parking places in neighborhoods, provided the residents choose this and it is feasible. Smart solutions should make the (new) neighborhoods green and also address dissatisfaction about too few parking spaces.

112392.explanation: Companies specifically ask for good MBO (middle vocational) schools. HBO (higher vocational) education is already available in the region.

112394.explanation: A committee of experienced experts is certainly needed now with the double aging population.

112333.explanation: For D66 Dijk en Waard, diversity and inclusion are important themes. Residents should be able to see themselves reflected in the municipal council and administration, which is why it is very important that the executive board reflects society.

112334.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard wants to strengthen local democracy by giving residents direct influence on policy. Through area-oriented working, stimulating citizen initiatives, and organizing citizen assemblies, we involve residents in shaping policy.

112337.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard wants all residents to be able to participate in the energy transition. We help residents to make sustainable changes through subsidies and the deployment of subsidy coaches and encourage residents to participate in local energy projects so that they benefit from generating sustainable energy.

112338.explanation: There is a shortage of business parks in the municipality. The local economy is hampered due to lack of space for business activities. Therefore, we must be more selective about the type of employment we want to attract. According to D66, distribution centers are not part of this.

112341.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard believes that the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves can continue, provided this is done responsibly, with attention to health and living environment. Due to harmful health effects during calm weather, we want to prohibit burning depending on weather conditions.

112346.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard supports the Dijk en Waard pass. Residents receiving social assistance benefits receive a credit from the municipality that they can spend on, among other things, sports, culture, and other social activities. This ensures that all residents can continue participating in society.

112347.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard finds it very important that the municipality has a local broadcaster and independent local media. This provides information about what is happening in the municipality and keeps residents involved.

112349.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard supports the Dijk en Waard pass. With this, we give residents with a limited income a credit they can spend on sports and culture. This way everyone can participate. However, D66 Dijk en Waard does not believe all residents should be eligible for such a scheme.

112352.explanation: In the past year, the BOA team has expanded significantly from 5 to 20. The BOAs themselves indicate that they are now sufficiently staffed. If developments in the coming years require more BOAs, D66 Dijk en Waard believes this investment in our safety should be made.

112353.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard prioritizes privacy. In general, we are against additional cameras because this only shifts problems elsewhere and violates residents' privacy. However, additional cameras may be placed in specific locations, such as around Middenwaard and the station.

112355.explanation: The BOAs themselves indicate that they currently do not find it necessary to arm themselves with batons. If developments in the coming years make it necessary to further equip BOAs, D66 Dijk en Waard will enable this.

112356.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard wants to build 10,000 homes, while the housing need for solely the Dijk en Waard population amounts to 5,000 homes. Housing shortage is a problem that transcends municipal borders. Therefore, D66 Dijk en Waard does not support a priority arrangement.

112358.explanation: Vacant properties during times of housing shortage are unacceptable. D66 Dijk en Waard wants the municipality to enable housing construction at such locations through changes in zoning plans and possibly subsidy grants.

112359.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard believes the municipality should solve the housing shortage with a combination of measures. We want to focus, among other things, on innovative forms of housing, such as tiny houses and container homes.

112360.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard finds it important that everyone gets the chance to participate. We must give everyone a chance to contribute to society in their own way. We therefore want to encourage people on social assistance to contribute, for example through volunteer work.

112364.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard wants to encourage sustainable modes of transport. We therefore focus on strengthening public transport and improving infrastructure for cyclists. Also, designating additional charging points for electric cars is part of our plans.

112365.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard wants to invest in infrastructure for cyclists. Where necessary, we will expand 30 kilometer zones for cars, making streets safer for pedestrians, cyclists, and playing children. However, we do not want to implement 30 kilometer zones throughout the entire built-up area.

112369.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard considers the waiting lists in youth care a major problem. This cannot be solved by purchasing care from the cheapest organizations. Young people with, for example, mental health issues deserve high-quality care. Good care should also lead to lower costs in the long term.

112370.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard believes that everyone should have access to basic products like sanitary pads and tampons. This is a matter of health, dignity, and equal opportunities. Combating period poverty must therefore become part of poverty policy.

112373.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard believes the municipality should make it attractive for tourists to visit our community. Increasing the tourist tax does not help this and therefore we are against it.

112376.explanation: Municipalities have a national task to accommodate refugees. Refugees therefore have the right to social housing, just like other priority groups. D66 believes it is important that refugees participate from day one. Language lessons and housing are essential for this.

112378.explanation: Climate change requires courage and decisiveness. D66 Dijk en Waard wants the municipality to be energy neutral by 2040. To achieve this, we allow solar panels on agricultural land. This way we contribute to a clean planet and make the energy supply in Dijk en Waard future-proof.

112383.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard finds it important to combat illegal drug trade. Coffeeshops therefore offer a solution and should not be prevented in advance. It is important to properly integrate a coffeeshop in the neighborhood and to monitor and prevent any nuisance.

112384.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard wants 30% of new homes to be social rental housing. This is important so that everyone in our municipality can find an affordable home.

112386.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard wants to tackle the housing shortage with innovative solutions. We therefore want to make home-sharing and home-splitting more accessible.

112387.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard attaches great value to village centers. They bring residents together and create social connections. Every district therefore has the right to a community center.

112389.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard believes there should be a balance in neighborhoods between parking spaces, greenery, and places where children can play and be active. Adding extra parking spaces disrupts this balance. At locations where new homes are built, we of course want to provide additional space.

112392.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard is proud of our vocational education (MBO). This way we retain talents skilled with their hands and technology in the municipality, and they can work for our companies after their training. To also keep theoretically trained people in Dijk en Waard, we want to establish a university of applied sciences (HBO) or department here.

112394.explanation: D66 Dijk en Waard finds the youth council very important because it represents a group that is not yet eligible to vote. Seniors can vote and can thus exert influence. Establishing a senior council is therefore not a priority for us.

112333.explanation: The primary concern is the administrative qualities of the aldermen. However, it is a good goal to achieve balanced representation. Extra effort may well be devoted to this.

112334.explanation: The CDA opposes referenda. The topics are often very complex; they increase and sharpen divisions in society without room for substantive discussion. The municipal council must weigh the interests of the majority and minority, especially on complex issues.

112337.explanation: Currently, the municipality already grants subsidies for sustainability, it is possible to take out a sustainability loan, and subsidies are available from the national government. Furthermore, many forms of sustainable energy pay for themselves within a reasonable time frame.

112338.explanation: We currently have a shortage of business parks. Distribution centers use relatively large amounts of scarce land and provide limited employment. We prefer to use the space for high-quality business activities.

112341.explanation: Nowadays, a wood stove or fireplace is also a way for many residents to keep energy bills manageable. Moreover, it is not the burning itself that is the problem, but the manner in which it is done. The CDA therefore sees more value in informing people about proper burning behavior.

112346.explanation: Sports are important and prevent many problems later in life. The CDA therefore believes sport should be accessible regardless of financial situation. We currently facilitate this via the DijkenwaardPas.

112347.explanation: The CDA recognizes the importance of the local broadcaster and supports it. However, we believe the current support is adequate and no extra funding is needed.

112349.explanation: Music is beneficial for children's development. Many families can pay for music lessons themselves. For families with less financial means, the CDA supports municipal assistance, as is currently done through the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture.

112352.explanation: In recent years, we have invested in the number of enforcement officers (BOA’s). We listen to their signals and can increase their numbers if needed. Additionally, the CDA supports deploying BOA’s who focus specifically on youth.

112353.explanation: Unfortunately, serious incidents still occur in our municipality. Camera surveillance can help in such situations. It can also provide protection in business parks and assist in investigations. It is always important to consider proportionality.

112355.explanation: The equipment of enforcement officers (BOA’s) is customized based on their own practical needs. Currently, they do not feel the need for batons.

112356.explanation: It is important that housing seekers have the chance to remain living in the municipality where they have their social network. Therefore, we support giving priority to people from the local municipality.

112358.explanation: Converting offices into housing can be a good solution in some cases. Each situation must be assessed to determine whether housing or office space is more appropriate at that location. It is up to the developer to make it a profitable development.

112359.explanation: Dijk en Waard is not a student city like Alkmaar, for example. Many students live relatively close by. Therefore, we oppose investing in temporary student housing, such as container homes. The municipality should focus on permanent affordable housing.

112360.explanation: The purpose of the counter-performance is to continue involving people in society. Fortunately, many people with benefits do not remain inactive. For them, requiring a counter-performance is therefore unnecessary.

112364.explanation: Charging points should be placed where they are needed. We do not support the municipality designating locations but favor a demand-driven approach.

112365.explanation: Our municipality features many separate bicycle and pedestrian paths. Therefore, in much of the municipality, reducing speed to 30 km/h is unnecessary.

112369.explanation: Especially in youth care, a thorough consideration of the choice of care provider is crucial. Cheap can certainly be 'expensive' here, which is unacceptable. Rogue care providers must also be excluded.

112370.explanation: Although it is important that no one gets into trouble because of menstruation costs, we believe general free distribution is not a core municipal task. Municipal resources can be better targeted to support residents who truly need it.

112373.explanation: The tourist tax in Dijk en Waard is currently 1 euro per person, per night. This is well below the Dutch average of 2.54 euros. Therefore, if necessary, an increase in the tourist tax would not be unrealistic.

112376.explanation: There is a legal obligation to arrange housing for status holders. Housing is important for good integration. This is often achieved by priority for social rental housing but can also be done through alternative temporary housing forms. If these exist, priority is no longer needed.

112378.explanation: It is very clear to us that we should be cautious with repurposing agricultural land because it is crucial for our food. Additionally, the municipality still has many roofs and parking lots where solar panels can be installed.

112383.explanation: There is a coffeeshop in Heerhugowaard; according to the CDA, this provides sufficient supply.

112384.explanation: Waiting lists for social rental housing are still far too long. Therefore, it is important to meet the obligation that at least 30% of new housing consists of social rental houses.

112386.explanation: Allowing room rental can increase pressure on residential neighborhoods, such as parking nuisance and reduced livability. It can also reduce the availability of family homes. The municipality must therefore balance housing supply with a pleasant, stable living environment for residents.

112387.explanation: The current offer of community centers is sufficient. Furthermore, the CDA believes that taxpayers’ money should not be invested in buildings but in activities. The community center should be run by the residents themselves, and municipal interference is not needed.

112389.explanation: For well-organized public spaces, it is important to have enough parking spaces but also sufficient greenery. At present, parking pressure in most districts is not so high that greenery should be sacrificed for extra parking.

112392.explanation: With InHolland nearby in Alkmaar, there is no need to establish a university of applied sciences in Dijk en Waard. Dijk en Waard is known for many and good vocational schools. As far as the CDA is concerned, we focus on becoming the vocational education capital.

112394.explanation: The group of seniors is growing, so it is important to properly hear the voice of this group. A "Senior Council" implies a new institution, which the CDA does not support. However, for example, "partner tables" are supported.

112333.explanation: It is good that there are both women and men in the executive board. We strive for a board that reflects our residents well. It is not necessary for there to be an equal number of men and women on the board.

112334.explanation: In the Netherlands, we use a low electoral threshold to have as many voices as possible in the municipal council. They have the mandate from the voters and must weigh all interests of our residents. A referendum, where you can only vote for or against, is not suitable for that.

112337.explanation: As ChristenUnie, we want to promote the generation of clean energy. The power grid is full, so we need more generation in our own region. We also receive funding from the national government for this. Therefore, we stimulate projects from residents that contribute significantly to this.

112338.explanation: The business parks in Dijk en Waard are less suitable for distribution centers that require a lot of space. We therefore encourage the establishment of companies that fit well here and contribute to employment for our residents.

112341.explanation: Many people suffer from wood smoke, as it contains fine particles-especially people with lung conditions. A complete ban is considered excessive. Therefore, we support limiting the nuisance. Providing information is important, for example about wood smoke filters that cause less nuisance.

112346.explanation: ChristenUnie Dijk en Waard has wholeheartedly cooperated with and agreed to the new Participation Policy. One important part of this is ensuring that residents with a minimum income and their children can also participate in sports or cultural activities.

112347.explanation: There is already a regulation obliging every municipality to maintain a local broadcaster. Dijk en Waard complies with this.

112349.explanation: Besides family and school, sports, music, and also theatre clubs are important for healthy development. Subsidies to examples like COOL make music lessons affordable and are therefore important.

112352.explanation: Fortunately, we have recently invested in more municipal enforcement officers (BOA's), for example to counter nuisance on the water. It is important that everyone feels safe on the streets, also in the evening. We must address nuisance caused by fatbikes.

112353.explanation: Camera surveillance is important in places where problems occur, for example at buildings where explosions or arson take place. Installing cameras everywhere only creates an illusion of safety and compromises privacy.

112355.explanation: BOA's are increasingly assigned police tasks and must be able to do their work well and safely. A baton can be a useful tool if BOA's need it to perform their tasks properly.

112356.explanation: In practice, social rental homes already go more than 50% to local residents. But former residents, for example those who have studied, should also have a chance to get housing. We have regional agreements to give our residents a chance in the region.

112358.explanation: It is very important that there are enough homes in Dijk en Waard but also sufficient jobs. Adequate space for businesses is therefore also very important. Currently, there is more of a shortage of space than vacancies.

112359.explanation: Temporary housing is relatively expensive and not permanent. That is why we find building affordable housing for all target groups more important and efficient. It also provides better living quality.

112360.explanation: It is important to encourage people with social benefits as much as possible to find suitable work. Working gives people the chance to develop themselves and to be meaningful. Coercion is not the right method.

112364.explanation: It is good that there are more electric cars. This also results in a healthier environment. This means that more charging points must be created. The municipality must respond to the specific demand of residents per neighborhood and maintain enough spots for non-electric cars.

112365.explanation: It appears that in cities where the maximum speed limit is reduced to 30 km/h, the number of (serious) accidents decreases significantly. Main roads can remain at 50 km/h, but for other roads, 30 km/h is a good standard.

112369.explanation: Price is very important, but so is quality. It is important that care is effective and does not cost more than necessary. We must prevent so-called care cowboys with poor care quality from receiving assignments.

112370.explanation: Period poverty can be very restricting, especially for young women. If they cannot afford those products, it can determine whether they can still participate at work or in social activities. It is therefore good that Dijk en Waard also participates in this.

112373.explanation: The tourist tax in Dijk en Waard is very low, namely 1 euro per overnight stay, especially compared to neighboring municipalities. This tax can be invested in facilities also used by tourists. A gradual increase is therefore justified.

112376.explanation: Refugees allowed to stay in the Netherlands must integrate into Dutch society as quickly as possible. Housing, work, and learning the Dutch language are very important for this. It is good that Dijk en Waard invests heavily in housing for all target groups.

112378.explanation: Currently, Dijk en Waard already has many solar panels, including on agricultural land. For a good energy mix, we need more wind turbines that provide energy particularly in autumn and winter. Solar panels can be installed on business premises to facilitate company establishment.

112383.explanation: It repeatedly appears how much drugs cause lasting damage to the brain development of young people. Drug use is also often the cause of traffic accidents. Drug sales are often linked to crime. Dijk en Waard already has enough coffeeshops and does not want more.

112384.explanation: There is a great need for social rental homes for, for example, starters, elderly people seeking smaller affordable homes and thus freeing up larger homes, but also for families. Given the enormous waiting list, 30% is the minimum in our view.

112386.explanation: It is fine that a landlord rents rooms, for example to students; housing shortages are severe. If houses in our residential neighborhoods are widely used for rental to migrants or for AirBnB, restrictions must be imposed because this is not good for social cohesion.

112387.explanation: ChristenUnie Dijk en Waard has strived this term for structural subsidies for neighborhood and village centers. These are meeting points for social activities or opportunities for coffee and a good conversation. This is especially important in light of increasing loneliness.

112389.explanation: Enough parking spaces were made in the residential centers during construction. However, some residents have converted their parking spaces into gardens, thereby reducing the number of spaces. It is very important that there is also enough greenery and playgrounds, so not more parking spaces.

112392.explanation: Dijk en Waard has good vocational education (MBO) facilities, which should align even better with the needs of our businesses where possible. We also need many skilled professionals! Alkmaar has good university of applied sciences (HBO) facilities. We should mainly focus on a good connection from MBO to HBO.

112394.explanation: Seniors in Dijk en Waard form a rapidly growing group. They often know better what is needed or desirable than (young) civil servants. Just as we have a Youth Council, a Seniors Council can also provide good advice to the municipality.

112333.explanation: The right person in the right place.

112338.explanation: Use of energy far too high.

112341.explanation: Fireplaces cause a lot of nuisance for people with respiratory issues.

112346.explanation: Already exists via Dijk en Waard Pass. No additional measures needed.

112349.explanation: Music is an important part of a child's development.

112352.explanation: Better for safety considering the number of residents and its growth.

112355.explanation: Due to the hardening of society, the enforcement officers need to be able to defend themselves.

112356.explanation: The housing needs of younger and older residents of our own municipality must be prioritized.

112359.explanation: There are too few homes for students, so this is an option.

112365.explanation: It must not become a second Amsterdam. This speed is really too low.

112369.explanation: Always watch the price because healthcare costs are enormously high.

112370.explanation: People must take care of their personal hygiene themselves.

112373.explanation: Tourists should contribute to municipal facilities.

112376.explanation: Our own residents are already on long waiting lists, and you want to make that even longer.

112378.explanation: We are against solar panels on meadows.

112383.explanation: The more you obstruct, the more illegality occurs.

112384.explanation: Houses are already unaffordable, and if you don't build socially, people get no chance at all.

112386.explanation: Distribution is fine. It has always been done as a kind of student house.

112387.explanation: It is very important to have good meeting centers in all neighborhoods.

112389.explanation: But it must not be at the expense of public green spaces.

112392.explanation: It is good for the development of the municipality and to retain knowledge in Dijk and Waard.

112394.explanation: We already have a client advisory council and that seems sufficient.

112333.explanation: Quality, experience, and support must lead in appointments. Diversity is valuable, but positions are not filled based on a mandatory quota or calculation model.

112334.explanation: Residents deserve a direct voice in major decisions. A referendum strengthens local democracy.

112337.explanation: We welcome initiatives from residents. Municipal support is possible if financially responsible, transparent, and demonstrably supported. No blank subsidies, but targeted support where residents take joint responsibility.

112338.explanation: Distribution centers can provide employment and economic growth. Opposing them outright is not right. Location, traffic pressure, and livability must be carefully assessed.

112341.explanation: Residents must have freedom in their own homes, but responsibility for nuisance or health risks. Enforcement only in case of nuisance due to misuse, no general ban that affects everyone.

112346.explanation: Sports are important, but the municipality should not routinely pay memberships. We help where necessary through targeted support, but personal responsibility remains the principle. Public funds must be used carefully and fairly.

112347.explanation: A strong, independent local broadcaster is important for transparency and informing residents. Good journalism strengthens local democracy. Support must be transparent and goal-oriented.

112349.explanation: Music education is valuable, but mandatory subsidies for every school are not the only way. Schools should have space for their own choices in their education budget.

112352.explanation: Safety is primarily a police task. However, enforcement officers can contribute to supervision and livability in neighborhoods. When extra effort is needed to strengthen safety and public order, the municipality should be able to invest in that.

112353.explanation: Camera surveillance is not a structural solution for safety issues. Safety requires sufficient police deployment, visible enforcement, and prevention. Drastic measures like extra cameras must remain exceptions. This way safety is taken seriously without excessive surveillance.

112355.explanation: Enforcement officers have a supervisory role. Heavy armament belongs with the police. Safety requires clear task division and professional deployment of the right services.

112356.explanation: Own residents must be fairly considered in the allocation of social housing when scarce.

112358.explanation: Addressing vacancy and adding homes is sensible. Subsidies can help enable transformation if targeted and leading to affordable housing.

112359.explanation: There is a housing shortage, also among youth and students. Temporary solutions can help relieve pressure, with clear conditions and a view on flow-through. This is a practical and temporary solution.

112360.explanation: Those who can work must contribute. Participation increases independence and involvement in society.

112364.explanation: Public parking spaces are scarce. Additional charging spots must not come at the expense of public parking spaces.

112365.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but no general 30 km/h speed limit.

112369.explanation: Quality and appropriate care for youth take precedence over the lowest price.

112370.explanation: Help with poverty is necessary, but through targeted arrangements, not general free distribution.

112373.explanation: Increasing charges is not a goal in itself. First use existing resources efficiently.

112376.explanation: Priority for social rental housing must be fair and balanced. The municipality has the legal task of accommodating status holders.

112378.explanation: Agricultural land is intended for food production.

112383.explanation: Not to be blocked outright. Establishment should depend on location, safety, and support within the neighborhood.

112384.explanation: Young people and starters must be able to continue living in Dijk en Waard. This requires sufficient affordable housing in social rental, middle rental, and affordable ownership. However, we find a fixed minimum percentage per project too rigid. Housing construction requires tailor-made solutions per location to keep projects feasible and to create the right mix per neighborhood.

112386.explanation: Independent homes must remain available for families, starters, the elderly, etc.

112387.explanation: Every village deserves its own meeting place to foster social cohesion.

112389.explanation: Accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs is essential. Parking standards must grow in line with the number of inhabitants and the homes to be built.

112392.explanation: Only if there is sufficient demand.

112394.explanation: The municipal council represents all residents. We support broad participation for everyone, not separate advisory councils for specific groups. This prevents administrative complexity and ensures that policies are weighed integrally instead of by individual interests.

statements.title: Dijk en Waard should get a seniors' council that can advise the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: A council of older residents that advises the municipality.

titleParts.text: krijgen die de gemeente advies kan geven.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain allowed in Dijk en Waard.

titleParts.text: In Dijk en Waard moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Dijk en Waard should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.text: In Dijk en Waard moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV camera surveillance in Dijk en Waard.

titleParts.text: In Dijk en Waard moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: A movable and temporary dwelling, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Residents of Dijk en Waard should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Dijk en Waard moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Dijk en Waard should be given batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Dijk en Waard controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should block the opening of a new coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Dijk en Waard should be social housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Dijk en Waard moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Every village in the municipality should have its own village/neighborhood center.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should lobby for a university of applied sciences (HBO) in Dijk en Waard.

titleParts.glossary: Influencing politics by talking to the decision-makers.

titleParts.text: voor een hbo-school in Dijk en Waard.
